'''Jack Welsby''' (born 17 March 2001) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb|so|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

==Background==
Welsby was born in Wigan, [[Greater Manchester]]. Playing for Shevington Sharks ARLFC as a child.

==Playing career==
===Club career===
In 2018, he made his [[Super League]] debut for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].
He played in the club's 8-4 [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull. In the final seconds of play with the scores at 4-4, St Helens attempted a field goal which rebounded off the goal post.  Welsby was the first player to the ball from the ricochet and he scored after the full-time siren to win the match for St Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league|title=St Helens v Wigan Warriors|publisher=BBC Sport|date=27 Nov 2020|access-date=27 Nov 2020}}</ref>

Welsby played for St Helens in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk|access-date=18 July 2021|archive-date=18 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210718102540/https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897|url-status=dead}}</ref>
In round 17 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Welsby scored a [[hat-trick]] in St Helens 42–10 over [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58062487|title=Hull FC 10-42 St Helens - Jack Welsby scores hat-trick in win|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
On 9 October, Welsby played for St Helens in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

In round 20 of the 2022 Betfred Super League season, Welsby kicked a drop goal in golden point extra-time to win the match for St Helens 13–12 against bottom placed [[Wakefield Trinity]].  In September, Welsby was awarded young player of the year at the 2022 Super League awards ceremony.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12658345/super-league-jack-welsby-lands-golden-point-winner-to-seal-st-helens-comeback-against-wakefield-trinity/|title=Super League: Jack Welsby lands golden-point winner to seal St Helens comeback against Wakefield Trinity|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

On 24 September, Welsby provided two try assists in St Helens 24-12 Grand Final victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>
In the [[2023 World Club Challenge]], Welsby scored the opening try for St Helens in their 13–12 upset victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite news |title=St Helens stun Penrith to win World Club Challenge |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64666651 |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In round 15 of the 2023 Super League season, Welsby made his 100th appearance for St Helens and scored two tries in the clubs 34–16 victory over rivals Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65822023|title=Super League: St Helens 34-16 Wigan Warriors - In-form hosts earn ruthless derby win|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
In round 20 of the 2023 Super League season, Welsby scored two tries for St Helens in their 22–18 victory over Leeds.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66301853|title=Super League: St Helens 22-18 Leeds Rhinos - Jack Welsby shines in intense encounter|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
Welsby played 29 games for St Helens in the 2023 Super League season scoring 11 tries as the club finished third on the table. Welsby played in St Helens narrow loss against the Catalans Dragons in the semi-final which ended St Helens four-year dominance of the competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23856044.paul-wellens-q-saints-review-year-look-2024/|title=Paul Wellens Q&A: Saints review of year and a look to 2024|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk|date=14 October 2023 }}</ref>

On 20 December 2023, Welsby signed a four-year contract extension to remain at St Helens until the end of the 2027 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/jack-welsby-reveals-why-he-rejected-nrl-offers-to-sign-st-helens-deal/|title=Jack Welsby reveals why he rejected NRL offers to sign St Helens deal|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

Welsby played 23 matches for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season including their golden point elimination playoff final loss against Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
‘Soul destroying’: Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

On 23 May 2025, Welsby scored a hat-trick in St Helens 46-4 win over Huddersfield.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cq54348ddvxo|title=Welsby hat-trick helps Saints thrash Giants|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 3 June 2025, it was announced that Welsby would miss at least 16 weeks with a knee injury. In round 22 of the 2025 Super League season, Welsby made his return to the St Helens side in their 52-4 win over Huddersfield.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/2025/06/03/injury-update-jack-welsby-lewis-murphy/|title=Injury update: Jack Welsby & Lewis Murphy|website=www.saintsrlfc.com}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2026 Super League season, Welsby injured his shoulder during St Helens disappointing loss against Warrington. It was later revealed that Welsby would miss four months and required surgery.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-jack-welsby-injury-2/|title=St Helens’ worst Jack Welsby fears realised as lengthy lay-off confirmed|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===International career===
On 15 October 2022, Welsby made his England debut against Samoa in the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] scoring one try and providing two assists as England won the match 60–6.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63271368|title=Rugby League World Cup: England 60-6 Samoa - Welsby, Young, Watkins, Farnworth score|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
Welsby played in all five matches for England at the tournament including the semi-final where he made a crucial handling error in golden point extra-time against Samoa. England would go on to lose the match 27–26 at the [[Emirates Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2022/nov/12/england-samoa-rugby-league-world-cup-semi-final-live/|title=England 26-27 Samoa: Rugby League World Cup semi-final – as it happened|newspaper=The Guardian|date=12 November 2022 |last1=Unwin |first1=Will }}</ref>

In the absence of regular captain [[George Williams (rugby league, born 1994)|George Williams]] through suspension, Welsby was named captain for the first {{rlnt|Tonga}} test on 22 October 2023, becoming the youngest ever captain.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Oct 2023|title=Jack Welsby to become England's youngest-ever captain in familiar territory; St Helens star 'extremely humbled'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jack-welsby-to-become-englands-youngest-ever-captain-in-familiar-territory-st-helens-star-extremely-humbled}}</ref>
Welsby played in all three tests as England won the series 3–0.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2023/nov/06/england-tonga-series-world-cup-huddersfield-st-helens-leeds/|title=England's 3-0 win over Tonga shows why they are the third best team|newspaper=The Guardian|date=6 November 2023 |last1=Willacy |first1=Gavin }}</ref>
